mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation through a lower discount rate, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy path is actively easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is declining for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 55% in easing windows over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in RSAFS indicates stronger household and business spending, which improves near-term revenue expectations and supports S&P 500 gains over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth exceeds 0.6% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered months with RSAFS growth above 0.6% do not produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if average triggered return is below the unconditional mean by any 3-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE reflects weakening labor demand and future consumption softness, which compresses earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average return after the trigger is nonnegative across any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL is stable, real output is expanding without a broad inflation shock, which supports margins and keeps policy pressure contained, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and flat CPIAUCSL fails to outperform the unconditional 60d SPX return in walk-forward tests, or if the interaction term becomes negative and insignificant for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals a cooling housing-demand impulse that often leads broader cyclical spending, so a sustained drop should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend HOUST readings are not followed by negative 5d SPX returns at a rate above 50%, or if the average 5d return after the trigger is positive in any 18-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A restrictive stance defined by FEDFUNDS above 4.5% transmits through higher discount rates and tighter credit conditions, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy is firmly above neutral.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show negative average 20d SPX returns, or if the directional accuracy of the trigger falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, payroll gains confirm broad labor-market strength that supports consumption and corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition fails to outperform the unconditional 60d SPX return, or if the spread between triggered and non-triggered returns is not positive in at least 2 of 3 rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 growth during a late-cycle expansion signals decelerating aggregate demand before earnings revisions turn negative, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP growth slowdowns are not followed by negative 20d SPX returns on average, or if the signal's walk-forward rank correlation is below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling segment.
